notes: This seed catalog is derived from the Netskope Vendor Field Catalog research report.
It is not a tenant export and must not be treated as proof of customer-specific configuration.
Inline Real-time Protection and API Data Protection are modeled separately through execution_mode.
App-specific activity support must be verified per tenant/app before marking a translation exact.

policy_objects.description: Determines which traffic reaches Netskope for inline real-time policy evaluation.
Inline policy surface for live web/cloud traffic using source, destination, activity, profile, action, and notification selectors.
Out-of-band SaaS/data-at-rest policy surface using object/resource selection, criteria, profiles, actions, notifications, and deferred follow-up actions.
Reusable detection bundle referenced by policies; may contain DLP rules, file profiles, ML classifiers, fingerprint rules, and exact match logic.
Rule-level detector logic using data identifiers, dictionaries, expressions, thresholds, and unique-count options.
Lowest-level detection input such as predefined identifier, keyword, regex, dictionary, weighted dictionary, or exact-match reference.
File metadata/profile object supporting file name/extension, file type, file hash, object ID, file size, protected/encrypted flags, and sensitivity labels.
Object used to distinguish sanctioned, unsanctioned, untagged, tenant-specific, personal, or enterprise app instances where supported.
policy_objects.console_location: Settings / App Instances or policy destination selectors

policy_objects.description: Reusable category selector, often backed by URL Lists, used to target custom destination sets in policies.
Exact or regex URL/IP list that can be attached to custom categories and later used in policy targeting.
User/admin notification template for alert/block/coaching workflows, including justification boxes, user actions, redirects, and variables.
Profile defining where forensic content is stored and whether it is encrypted.
Profile defining quarantine destination app/instance/folder/owner, encryption, admin notifications, and tombstone behavior.
Profile/action family for legal-hold preservation. Existence is confirmed, but detailed public field schema requires tenant/API verification.
Downstream incident/evidence record created by DLP profiles, rules, file profiles, classifiers, fingerprints, or actions.

activities.description: Represents web/app browsing or access behavior. Public docs note browse has special event behavior and may not generate alert events in some policy contexts.
limitations: Browse behavior differs from content activities and may not always appear as a policy event.

activities.description: Authentication-related activity family confirmed in Universal Connector documentation.
limitations: Login visibility depends on connector/app support.

activities.description: Submission activity for posted form content; Universal/Web Universal connector supports Formpost with DLP only.
limitations: Activity extraction depends on app/connector; GenAI prompt mapping must be validated by app.

activities.description: App-specific post/comment/content-submission activity, depending on app connector support.
limitations: No single global public activity enum guarantees Post across all selected apps/categories.

activities.description: File upload or attachment submission activity. Widely used inline primitive and relevant for GenAI attachments/uploads.
limitations: App/category selection may show union of activity support; selected app may not support Upload.

activities.description: File download activity. Relevant for file movement and some API/data-at-rest contexts.
limitations: Should not be assumed for GenAI submission policies unless explicitly present in the policy intent.

activities.description: AI-generated response resource type/activity in GenAI API monitoring, publicly confirmed for certain enterprise GenAI integrations such as Microsoft Copilot and Claude/ChatGPT enterprise monitored activities.
limitations: Do not assume inline response inspection across all GenAI apps; validate per app and control plane.

activities.description: Collaboration or sharing-related activity/resource criteria, more common in API Data Protection.
limitations: Support varies by SaaS app/resource type.

activities.description: Object update activity in app-specific or API-monitored contexts.
limitations: Must be validated against the selected app/resource type.

activities.description: Object creation event in API or app-specific monitoring.
limitations: Often app/resource-specific.

activities.description: Object deletion activity in API/app-specific contexts.
limitations: Not normally used for GenAI DLP submission controls.

activities.description: Copy/paste support is confirmed in specific Google Suite inline DLP contexts with minimum character requirements.
limitations: Do not treat as universal; Google Suite docs mention 50 character minimum for paste inspection.

activities.description: Print-related control appears as an API Data Protection remediation/action capability in some contexts.
limitations: Typically an API/remediation control, not a generic inline GenAI activity.

actions.description: Permits the activity/content; often used as explicit allow or default pass-through behavior.
limitations: Broad allow policies must be ordered carefully to avoid bypassing downstream DLP.

actions.description: Generates alert/incident visibility without preventing user action where supported.
limitations: Alert on Browse may not generate alert events in some documented contexts.

actions.description: Shows user notification/coaching and may allow the user to proceed or stop depending on template/action design.
limitations: Reverse proxy blocked apps may show application error instead of block template in some cases.

actions.description: Prevents the user activity or policy match. Can use notification templates and may optionally suppress alerts.
limitations: Block behavior depends on access method/app; app/page events may still be generated even if alerts are suppressed.

actions.description: Moves content to a quarantine destination/profile where supported.
limitations: Inline quarantine is available only when DLP is in the policy and only for upload activity.
Inline encryption is available only when an app instance of a cloud app is selected and only for upload activity.

actions.description: Preserves matching content for legal hold workflows.
limitations: Detailed profile fields are not fully verified from public docs in the research pass.

actions.description: Applies a sensitivity label through API Data Protection workflows where supported.
limitations: Action support varies by app; selecting all applications may disable actions not supported across all apps.

actions.description: Restricts sharing, revokes public/org-wide/user access, sets link expiration, or limits view/download rights in API Data Protection where supported.
limitations: Action support varies significantly by SaaS app and resource type.

actions.description: Deletes matching resource/content through API Data Protection where supported.
limitations: High-impact action; support varies by app and should require explicit review.

detection_objects.description: Reusable profile containing DLP rules, file profiles, ML classifiers, and fingerprint/exact match components.
limitations: Policy can only enforce what the underlying profile/rules detect.

detection_objects.description: DLP logic layer with rules, severity thresholds, record/aggregate score modes, unique-count options, and expressions.
limitations: Rules within a condition are ANDed; conditions are ORed across the profile.

detection_objects.description: Predefined identifier, keyword, regex, or other low-level entity used by DLP rules.
limitations: Complex validation and filters such as Luhn/Common-Sense must be captured at rule/entity level.

detection_objects.description: Keyword or regex dictionary, including weighted dictionary behavior.
limitations: Weights can affect aggregate score; not the same as simple keyword matching.

detection_objects.description: Composes identifiers with AND, OR, NOT, NEAR, and GDI-style expressions.
limitations: NEAR has a documented maximum of 1000 characters.

detection_objects.description: Exact-match dataset using uploaded CSV/TXT with headers, normalization, column groups, and SHA-256 hashing.
limitations: File upload path has size constraints; larger datasets may require virtual appliance path and tenant validation.

detection_objects.description: Matches file name/extension, file type, file hash, object ID, file size, protected/encrypted flags, and sensitivity labels.
limitations: Some attributes are OR-style while size/protected/label constraints are AND-style; model carefully.

detection_objects.description: File Profile sensitivity label matching using vendor, instance, and label selection, including files with/without labels from an instance.
limitations: Requires metadata visibility and correct label/instance mapping.

detection_objects.description: File Profile matching on name or extension, usable for filename-driven controls if configured explicitly.
limitations: Do not translate filename intent into broad DLP block without a file profile condition.

detection_objects.description: ML-based file classifier referenced by DLP profiles/incidents.
limitations: Detailed public field schema not fully verified in the research pass.

detection_objects.description: Fingerprint detection rule referenced in DLP profiles/incidents.
limitations: Detailed public field schema requires tenant/API validation.

app_scope_objects.description: Netskope application selector for cloud/SaaS apps.
limitations: Activity support depends on connector/app.

app_scope_objects.description: Netskope category selector, including predefined categories.
limitations: Category-level activity selector may show union support that individual apps do not all support.

app_scope_objects.description: Customer-defined category, often backed by URL Lists, selectable in Real-time Protection categories.
limitations: Requires correct URL list/category maintenance; may require SWG licensing.

app_scope_objects.description: Exact or regex URL/IP list used to build custom categories.
limitations: Typically not a direct inline DLP policy field; used through custom categories.

app_scope_objects.description: Specific app instance / tenant / account selector used to distinguish enterprise vs personal or sanctioned vs unsanctioned contexts.
limitations: Some detailed profile fields require tenant validation.

app_scope_objects.description: Tag-based app instance grouping, including sanctioned/unsanctioned/untagged states.
limitations: Correct tagging and instance discovery are prerequisites for accurate policy scope.

app_scope_objects.description: Netskope Cloud Confidence Index app tag used as a destination/app selector in policies.
limitations: Mapping from generic tags to exact GenAI governance categories may be lossy.

app_scope_objects.description: API Data Protection selector for specific resource IDs or resource filters.
limitations: Resource type and ID semantics vary by connected SaaS app.

limitations.title: Activity support varies by app and connector
limitations.description: The visible activity list can be the union of activities supported by selected apps/categories; not every selected app supports every activity.
limitations.impact: Translator must not assume Post/Upload/Download/Response are always available for a category.
limitations.recommended_warning: Validate activity support for the selected app/category before marking this mapping exact.
limitations.validation_method: Tenant UI activity support dialog, app connector docs, or policy test.

limitations.title: Browse activity has special event behavior
limitations.description: Browse is visible in monitoring contexts but may not behave like DLP content activities and may not generate alert events in some configurations.
limitations.impact: Access-control translations should treat browse differently from content submission activities.
limitations.recommended_warning: Browse/access policy should be validated with expected event/alert behavior.
limitations.validation_method: Policy test and event log review.

limitations.title: GenAI response inspection is not universally verified for inline policies
limitations.description: Public docs confirm AI response/resource monitoring in certain API/enterprise GenAI contexts, but this should not be assumed for all inline GenAI traffic.
limitations.impact: Response activity should not be added to translated policies unless the neutral policy explicitly requires it and the vendor capability is verified.
limitations.recommended_warning: AI response inspection requires app/control-plane validation.
limitations.validation_method: Tenant test with target GenAI app and policy surface.

limitations.title: Generic GenAI category mapping is lossy
limitations.description: Mapping a specific governance category such as Prohibited or Restricted/Unassessed to a generic Generative AI category loses policy intent.
limitations.impact: Translator should mark generic category mapping as lossy unless a customer-specific app category/object mapping exists.
limitations.recommended_warning: Customer vendor mapping is required for exact GenAI category scope.

limitations.title: SSL Do Not Decrypt can prevent inspection
limitations.description: Traffic that is not decrypted or is bypassed may not be eligible for full DLP inspection.
limitations.impact: DLP profile/action may not trigger even if policy exists.
limitations.recommended_warning: Confirm SSL inspection and DND exceptions before verifying this policy.
limitations.validation_method: Steering config review and live test.

limitations.title: Certificate-pinned apps may bypass inspection
limitations.description: Some apps/clients may not tolerate standard inspection and can require bypass or special handling.
limitations.impact: Inline DLP controls may not apply to pinned client traffic.
limitations.recommended_warning: Validate traffic path for certificate-pinned clients before relying on inline inspection.
limitations.validation_method: Client traffic test and connection logs.

limitations.title: Filename detection requires a real file profile condition
limitations.description: Filename intent must translate into File Profile name/extension matching rather than a broad DLP policy with no condition.
limitations.impact: Incorrect translation can overblock all uploads.
limitations.recommended_warning: Filename detection requires configured File Profile matching the filename pattern.
limitations.validation_method: Verify File Profile configuration and upload test.

limitations.title: Sensitivity label detection requires metadata visibility
limitations.description: Label detection depends on file metadata visibility and correct vendor/instance/label mapping.
limitations.impact: Label-based controls may not trigger if metadata is missing, stripped, encrypted, or unmapped.
limitations.recommended_warning: Validate label metadata detection with real labelled test files.
limitations.validation_method: Upload labelled file and confirm incident/policy match.

limitations.title: Broad allow policies can bypass downstream controls
limitations.description: Allow/bypass policies can suppress or bypass downstream inspection if ordered or scoped incorrectly.
limitations.impact: Approved-use translations require app instance/user group validation and policy-order review.
limitations.recommended_warning: Review policy order and scope before enabling allow policies.
limitations.validation_method: Policy order review and negative test.

limitations.title: API and inline controls use different object models
limitations.description: Inline Real-time Protection and API Data Protection use overlapping but distinct selectors, actions, resources, and workflows.
limitations.impact: Translator must not reuse inline fields blindly for API policies or vice versa.
limitations.recommended_warning: Execution mode must be selected before mapping fields.
limitations.validation_method: Check execution_mode and vendor surface in translated output.

prerequisites.title: Traffic steering configured
prerequisites.description: Target traffic must be steered to Netskope for inline Real-time Protection evaluation.
prerequisites.why_it_matters: Unsteered traffic cannot be inspected or enforced by inline policies.
prerequisites.validation_method: Review steering configuration and connection/app event logs.

prerequisites.title: SSL inspection/decryption enabled where required
prerequisites.description: Content DLP requires visibility into traffic/content; DND exceptions can suppress inspection.
prerequisites.why_it_matters: Without inspection, post/upload data may not be evaluated.
prerequisites.validation_method: Review SSL policy and test sample upload/prompt.

prerequisites.title: App activity support verified
prerequisites.description: Selected app/category must support the translated activities.
prerequisites.why_it_matters: A policy selecting unsupported activities will not enforce as expected.
prerequisites.validation_method: Use tenant activity support view or run positive activity test.

prerequisites.title: Customer app/category mapping exists
prerequisites.description: Governance categories must map to real Netskope app categories/app lists/custom categories for exact translation.
prerequisites.why_it_matters: Generic Generative AI mapping is lossy and may over/under-scope policies.

prerequisites.title: DLP profile created and referenced
prerequisites.description: Content detection policies require an existing DLP profile/rule structure.
prerequisites.why_it_matters: Policy cannot enforce content detection without a detector profile.
prerequisites.validation_method: Check DLP profile name/ID in tenant and run positive test.

prerequisites.title: Notification/coaching template created
prerequisites.description: Coach, user alert, block with justification, or acknowledgement workflows require configured templates.
prerequisites.why_it_matters: User-facing behavior cannot match the intended decision without a template.
prerequisites.validation_method: Verify template exists and contains required user action/justification fields.

prerequisites.title: Policy order reviewed
prerequisites.description: Netskope policy order can affect which policy/profile/action applies first.
prerequisites.why_it_matters: Higher-priority allow/bypass policies may suppress intended controls.
prerequisites.validation_method: Review translated policy order and test negative/positive cases.

prerequisites.title: App instance mapping verified
prerequisites.description: App instance/tenant mapping is required for exact personal vs enterprise separation.
prerequisites.why_it_matters: User-based allow may accidentally permit personal/consumer instances if not separated.
prerequisites.validation_method: Verify app instance selector and test enterprise vs personal login.

prerequisites.title: Forensic profile configured if evidence is required
prerequisites.description: Evidence preservation requires forensic/evidence storage settings where used.
prerequisites.why_it_matters: Policy may block/alert without preserving expected forensic evidence.
prerequisites.validation_method: Verify forensic profile and incident evidence download path.

prerequisites.title: API connector enabled for SaaS/API scanning
prerequisites.description: Out-of-band API Data Protection requires connected SaaS application/API integration.
prerequisites.why_it_matters: API policies cannot scan resources without an enabled connector.
prerequisites.validation_method: Check API connector status and scan event generation.

test_requirements.title: Inline access block validation
test_requirements.test_step: Attempt browse/login/access to a destination in the blocked app category.
test_requirements.expected_result: Access is blocked or controlled according to the translated Netskope action, and the expected event/log is visible.

test_requirements.title: Inline upload DLP positive test
test_requirements.test_step: Upload a synthetic file containing matching DLP content to the scoped app/category.
test_requirements.expected_result: Translated DLP profile matches and configured action occurs.

test_requirements.title: Inline post/formpost DLP positive test
test_requirements.test_step: Submit synthetic sensitive content through a prompt/form/post activity in the scoped app.
test_requirements.expected_result: DLP evaluates the submission and the configured action occurs.

test_requirements.title: Sensitivity label detection test
test_requirements.test_step: Upload a correctly labelled test file matching the configured sensitivity label.
test_requirements.expected_result: File Profile/sensitivity label condition matches and expected policy action occurs.

test_requirements.title: Filename pattern detection test
test_requirements.test_step: Upload a file whose name matches the configured filename/file profile pattern.
test_requirements.expected_result: Filename/file profile condition matches without triggering broad content-only policy behavior.

test_requirements.title: GenAI category scope negative test
test_requirements.test_step: Attempt activity against an app outside the intended governance category.
test_requirements.expected_result: Policy does not trigger for out-of-scope app if customer app-category mapping is exact.

test_requirements.title: Policy order and allow-bypass negative test
test_requirements.test_step: Test a blocked/controlled sample when higher-priority allow policies are present.
test_requirements.expected_result: Higher-priority allow policy does not bypass required DLP control unless intentionally scoped.

test_requirements.title: API GenAI response/resource test
test_requirements.test_step: Generate or update an AI prompt/response resource in a supported enterprise GenAI API integration.
test_requirements.expected_result: API Data Protection detects the resource type only where vendor/app support is verified.

test_requirements.title: Forensic evidence preservation test
test_requirements.test_step: Trigger a DLP policy requiring evidence preservation and verify forensic/original file availability.
test_requirements.expected_result: Forensic content or original file can be retrieved according to configured profile.
